content: The core performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and dedicated curious agents that existing literature explores can be explained by how each method interacts with episodic active memory storage in 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents: ICM and RND treat novelty prediction as a continuous secondary reward, but they do not prioritize retaining high-uncertainty exploratory observations in long-term agent memory the way structured active learning does. A hybrid curious agent that uses SOTA active memory pruning to only retain the most uncertain exploratory trajectories for intrinsic reward calculation will outperform both standalone ICM/RND and unstructured curious agents on sparse-reward long-horizon tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
